//: Day-1 phone tutorial (docs/design/day1_phone_tutorial.md, Opus 4.8 -> Sonnet 5 Stage 2). The mechanical 'how to play' thread -- orthogonal to hs_onboarding_events.json's STORY beats; this batch teaches the UI (phone tabs, Time bar, Plan Today, game days, Bank/needs, saving) and tells no story. 4 events, all scope:'avatar', weight 1.0, all carrying { field: tier, op: ==, value: 0 } (self-suppresses the moment an avatar graduates HS, same idiom as the onboarding arc), all contact:'getting_started', all category:'general'. File name sorts after core_events.json and before every hs_* batch, so once a beat is eligible it wins that day's one-fire-per-subject slot ahead of the narrative arc -- tut_welcome has no gate but tier==0+!tut_welcomed, so it wins day 2 outright and hs_first_day shifts to day 3 (design doc §2.3/§7); links 2-4 carry min_days_since:2 so the chain forces a one-day gap that alternates with the onboarding chain instead of monopolizing the slot (§7.2: tut_welcome@2, hs_first_day@3, tut_daily_loop@4, hs_meet_coach@5, tut_game_day@6, hs_tryouts@7, tut_wrap@8). Consequences are flags only -- deliberately no person_stat/funds/stress/relationship anywhere in this batch; a how-to-play tutorial must never reward or penalize reading it (§3.3), which keeps it trivially calibration-inert (no MC/Simulation touch, no re-band possible). Skip path: tut_welcome's third choice sets all four end-flags at once, short-circuiting the whole chain in one tap -- the sanctioned dismissal for veterans and re-exposed gen-2 heirs (a fresh tier-0 avatar re-qualifies per-flag; §6.4). New contact: 'getting_started' in contacts.json, role 'unknown' -- the design doc allowed role:'system' PENDING a check against ContactJson's role handling; that check found the loader's role switch is closed (throws FormatException on an unrecognized string, Assets/Narrative/Contacts/ContactRegistry.cs's ContactJson.ParseContact), so per the design doc's own contingency (§3.2) this ships on the existing 'unknown' role instead of adding a new enum member -- zero engine touch, exactly as promised. DEVIATION from the design doc's near-final copy (§5): the doc's authoring spec lists TWO delayed texts on one choice each of tut_welcome/tut_daily_loop/tut_game_day's first choice, but a choice's 'text_message' is a SINGULAR field in the engine (GrittyEventJson.cs ~L292-335: one string or one {body,delay_days} object per choice, no array) -- there is no shipped or engine precedent for multiple texts off one choice. Reconciled by merging each pair into one message body at delay_days:1 (keeps both sentences' content, keeps the 'fills in over the following day or two' spread the doc's own §8 constants table calls for, since the event-level fire-time text already lands day 0). All other choices carry the doc's copy verbatim.

events.prompt: New phone, new life. This thing is how you run all of it. Up top are your tabs — Events is where moments like this land, Messages is where your texts live (including these tips). Swipe around whenever you're lost.
events.text_message: Welcome. I'll drop you a few tips here over the next couple days — this thread stays put if you want to reread it.

choices.label: Show me around
choices.outcome: You thumb through the tabs. It's a lot, but it's all in one place.

text_message.body: Events = story + choices. Messages = texts like this. Calendar shows your season; Bank is money, needs, and bills. No rush to master it — the next tip covers how a day actually moves.

choices.label: Just the essentials
choices.outcome: Fine — the short version it is.

text_message.body: Two tabs matter most at first: Events (this kind of card) and Bank (your money and needs). We'll cover the rest as it comes up.

choices.label: I've played before — skip the tips
choices.outcome: Say no more. You know where everything is.

text_message.body: You're all set. This thread's here if you ever want it.

events.prompt: Time moves on its own now. The bar up top runs the clock — Pause it, set Slow / Normal / Fast, or hit Skip Day to jump straight to tomorrow. Before a day rolls over, open Plan Today to decide how you spend it.
events.text_message: The clock ticks in real time — Fast burns a day in a couple minutes, Skip Day ends it now.

choices.label: Walk me through planning a day
choices.outcome: You open Plan Today and slide the hours around until the day adds up.

text_message.body: Plan Today splits your hours across Sleep, Practice, Work, and Free time. School hours are locked in on school days — the card folds them in for you. Watch the 'Free hours' line — if it says you're over, trim a block before you Confirm.

choices.label: Just the clock, thanks
choices.outcome: You get the hang of the bar — pause, speed up, skip. Simple.

text_message.body: Paused, the world holds. It also auto-pauses when something needs you — a choice, a game, a deal.

choices.label: Got it
choices.outcome: You've got the daily rhythm. Onward.

events.prompt: Game days are marked on your Calendar. When one lands, the Time bar swaps in a Play Game button — tap it to step into the at-bat yourself, or Skip Day to let the team play it out. Afterward, League has the standings and Player has your stat line.
events.text_message: Game day soon. Play it live for the at-bats, or skip and let it sim — your call every time.

choices.label: How does the at-bat work?
choices.outcome: You picture stepping in — read the pitch, pick your moment.

text_message.body: At the plate you read the pitch and choose how to attack it. Your ratings do the rest. It's optional every game — skipping just autopilots it. Check the Player tab after to see how the line moved, and League for where the team sits.

choices.label: I'll just sim them for now
choices.outcome: You decide to let the team handle the games for a while. That's fine.

text_message.body: Skip Day plays your games for you — no penalty. Jump in live whenever you want the bat.

events.prompt: Last one. The Bank tab is your money, your five needs (keep them off the floor), and the weekly cost-of-living bill. Settings has Save Now, though the game saves itself as you go. That's the whole loop — now go live it.
events.text_message: You're set. Keep your needs up, keep an eye on the bills, and the rest is your story to write.

choices.label: Thanks — I'm set
choices.outcome: You pocket the phone and get on with it.

text_message.body: That's everything. This thread stays here if you need a refresher. Good luck out there.

choices.label: What happens if a need bottoms out?
choices.outcome: You make a mental note to keep an eye on the meters.

text_message.body: Let a need crater and it starts dragging on everything else — eat, sleep, wash up, see people, stay fit. The Bank tab shows all five.
